forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KW26C3MSTK5T2QVE4XBV9CH8
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c080
+project: expect
+title: browser surface adapter (CDP a11y via chromiumoxide)
+---
+## What
+Add the `browser` surface: drive and observe a web UI through the accessibility tree via CDP — pure Rust, no Node/Playwright. Per `ideas/expect.md` §"Surface adapters" (browser row) and §"Accessibility is the GUI's drive and observe channel".
+
+- New `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surface/browser.rs` implementing `SurfaceAdapter`:
+  - In-process mechanism: **CDP** `Accessibility` + `Input` via `chromiumoxide` (pure Rust). Provision launches/attaches to Chromium; teardown closes it.
+  - Drive: press/type by `role[name=…]` (CDP `Input`). Observe: snapshot the a11y tree (CDP `Accessibility`).
+  - Locator dialect: `role[name=…]` + tree relationship (`within`/`ancestor`); a11y-stable, NOT pixels. A control rename surfaces as honest structural drift. Extend the assertion compiler for the a11y locator dialect.
+  - Sparse a11y → vision/OCR is an explicit last resort (note, don't build now); a sparse tree is itself a signal.
+- Deterministic surface: mechanical a11y actuation is reproducible (reclassifies browser alongside cli/http) — runs once by default; non-determinism only via the agent fallback.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Against a fixture web page, the adapter presses a `button[name="…"]` and snapshots the resulting a11y tree.
+- [ ] `role[name=…]` + `within`/`ancestor` locators bind and evaluate; a renamed control surfaces as structural drift.
+- [ ] No Node and no Playwright — chromiumoxide/CDP only.
+- [ ] Teardown closes the browser.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test serving a tiny static HTML fixture, driving a button by role+name and asserting an observed a11y node value. (Gate behind a feature/availability check if Chromium isn't present in CI; document the skip.)
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect browser` passes (or skips cleanly without Chromium).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file

Next: chromiumoxide adapter in surface/browser.rs with a runtime-gated integration test.'

+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw4ktwbwkw85vxt38n52w0cs
+  text: 'Browser adapter landed. crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surface/browser.rs implements SurfaceAdapter via chromiumoxide CDP (pure Rust, no Node/Playwright): provision launches Chromium (gated on detection::default_executable) + navigates; drive presses/types by role[name=…] via CDP Input (getFullAXTree -> backend node -> box-model center -> dispatchMouseEvent; type via DOM.focus + Input.insertText); observe snapshots the a11y tree via Accessibility.getFullAXTree -> A11yNode; teardown closes the browser + aborts the handler task. resolves_mechanically returns false for unparseable steps (agent fallback). Integration test tests/browser.rs serves a static HTML fixture via axum and is GATED on BrowserAdapter::chromium_available() (skips+logs if absent). On this machine Chromium WAS present so the e2e press+observe path actually ran green (3.7s). Verification: cargo nextest -p swissarmyhammer-expect = 293 passed/0 failed (105 in the targeted browser/a11y/surface/assertion filter); cargo check --workspace OK; clippy -p swissarmyhammer-expect --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo fmt applied. Note: chromiumoxide was already declared in [workspace.dependencies]; just wired it into the crate. Leaving task in doing for review.'
+  timestamp: 2026-06-27T13:20:49.532471+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KW26C3MSTK5T2QVE4XBV9CH8
 position_column: doing

   timestamp: 2026-06-27T13:20:49.532471+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw4ma58547h10vahem3vwt7m
+  text: 'Adversarial double-check ran (verdict REVISE, all findings low/informational; acceptance criteria confirmed met). Acted on all three: (1) drive dialect now parses the selector strictly via new A11ySelector::parse_exact — a trailing within/ancestor scope or stray token no longer silently drops and presses the wrong control (added unit test rejects_a_press_with_trailing_scope_or_garbage); (2) provision error path now closes+waits the browser and aborts the handler if new_page/wait_for_navigation fails after launch, so a failed provision can''t leak a Chromium process (open_page helper); (3) documented that count phrasing is not honored for a11y locators (a11y_intent doc). Re-verified: cargo fmt OK, clippy -p swissarmyhammer-expect --all-targets -D warnings clean, cargo nextest -p swissarmyhammer-expect = 294 passed/0 failed (browser e2e test ran green against local Chromium). Task left in doing for /review.'
+  timestamp: 2026-06-27T13:29:10.149805+00:00

 - 01KW26C3MSTK5T2QVE4XBV9CH8
 position_column: doing
